terms.definition: Ahead-of-Time compilation. IL compiled to native code before runtime. .NET 8 supports Native AOT.
Base Class Library. Core standard libraries in System.*.
Converting a value type to object or an interface. Causes heap allocation.
Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance — distributed systems can only guarantee two.
Common Language Runtime. The virtual machine that executes .NET IL code.
Common Language Specification. Subset of CTS that ensures cross-language interop.
Command Query Responsibility Segregation. Separate read and write models.
Common Type System. Defines all types in .NET across languages.
Domain-Driven Design. Design software around the business domain with aggregates, entities, value objects.
Dependency Injection. Providing dependencies from outside rather than constructing them inside.
Garbage Collector. Automatic memory management. Generational: Gen 0, 1, 2, LOH, POH.
Intermediate Language (also CIL). Compiled C# output. JIT-compiled at runtime.
Builds expression trees translated to SQL. Use with EF Core for DB queries.
Just-In-Time compiler. Converts IL to native machine code at runtime.
Large Object Heap. For objects ≥ 85,000 bytes. Infrequently collected.
Like Span<T> but heap-allocatable. Can be used in async methods.
ASP.NET Core request pipeline components. Order matters.
Nullable Reference Types (C# 8+). Compiler annotations, not runtime null checks.
Object-Relational Mapper. Maps .NET objects to database tables. EF Core is the primary .NET ORM.
Pinned Object Heap (.NET 5+). For objects pinned in memory (interop, unsafe code).
Stack-allocated slice over contiguous memory. Zero allocation. ref struct.
Task-based Asynchronous Pattern. async/await + Task<T>. The standard async model in .NET.
Allocation-free alternative to Task<T> when result is often synchronous.
